feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 150 to 200 mm of rain about the ranges, and 60 to 90 mm nearer the coast. Peak rates of 10 to 20 mm/h expected Monday night and Tuesday morning about the ranges. Freezing level about 1800 metres. Note, lesser amounts of rain are expected to persist into Tuesday evening and through Wednesday, which could make for a significant amount of rain for the region. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, amounts may approach warning criteria. Note this rain falling as snow above 1000 metres, likely reaching as low as 800 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeasterly winds may approach severe gale about exposed valleys. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy snow above 800 metres, where snowfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Lesser amounts of snow to 400 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: The chance of upgrade for this Watch has been decreased to Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Expect 1 to 3 cm of snow to settle on the road from this afternoon until early tomorrow morning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow about the road this afternoon until Monday afternoon. 5 to 10 cm may accumulate on the road above 300 metres, but possibly as much as 30 cm near the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow is expected to continue about the road above 400 metres. Expect another 15 to 25 cm near the summit with 2 to 5 cm down to 400 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected about and eastwards of the summit. 4 to 6 cm may accumulate above 400 metres and 6 to 10 cm near the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Red
entry.summary: Expect 80 to 120 mm of rain. The heaviest falls are likely from late Sunday afternoon until early Monday morning when intensities of 10 to 15 mm/h are possible. Note, snow level lowering and snow is possible above 200 metres inland, and there is a separate snow warning. This is a significant amount of rain for low lying areas, especially impactful around low lying parts of Dunedin and the Taieri Plains. Impact: Threat to life from dangerous river conditions, significant flooding and slips. Conditions will disrupt travel, make some roads impassable, and isolate communities. Changes: Upgraded from a Watch/Orange Warning to a Red Warning for the coastal parts of Dunedin and South Otago.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 80 to 120 mm of rain. The heaviest falls are likely from late Sunday afternoon until early Monday morning when intensities of 10 to 15 mm/h are possible. Note, snow level lowering and snow is possible above 200 metres inland, and there is a separate snow warning. Impact: Dangerous river conditions, flooding, and slips likely. Dangerous driving conditions and road closures are expected. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: High.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain and accumulations may approach warning criteria. Note, snow level lowering and there is a separate warning for heavy snowfall for northern Southland. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Snow is expected to develop Sunday morning and the level lowers during the day, possibly down to 200 metres in places. Between 400 and 600 metres, 15 to 30 cm of snow is expected, especially south of Queenstown to Middlemarch, but accumulations may exceed 1 metre above 800 metres altitude. Impact: Travel disruption and damage to trees and powerlines possible. Cold conditions may cause stress for livestock. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate. Changes: The chance of upgrade for this Orange Warning has been increased to Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
